hi again! i'm actually eyeing the thea asymmetrical bottoms
i'm going btwn sizes and am unsure of which one to commit too
Blackbough Swim: If you're between sizes, I recommend going with the larger size. You can also check our size chart here: https://blackbough.attn.tv/as-u4Zspnk5Z.
i figured to size up bc that's what i did for the other bottoms i purchased
but i was going through the comments and two of them have said that this particular style runs on the larger size... so possibly size down?
Blackbough Swim: The Thea Asymmetrical Cheeky Bottoms may run larger. If you're between sizes, consider sizing down. For more details, check the product page: https://blackbough.attn.tv/aCpcjFwc2m9R
just not sure bc this is one that cannot be exchanged so i'm a bit iffy on which one to get

Blackbough Swim: I understand your hesitation and I'll be happy to help you find the right size! Upon checking further, it is stated on the product page that Thea Asymmetrical Cheeky Bottoms runs true to size. For additional reference, one of our models is 5'7", with a 33B bust 24" waist, and 34" hips, wearing a size Small. Another one is 5'9", with a 36C bust 28" waist, 44" hips wearing a size XL. I hope this helps. Please let me know if there's anything else I can assist you with.
i know it's not an exact science or anything but as someone who is a 29" waist and got a large in the Lydia Classic Medium Bottoms, which size would you recommend?
Blackbough Swim: Could you please share how the Lydia Classic Medium Bottoms fit you?
through a picture?
the large fit rather comfortably! not tight at all and hugs the hips well :)

Blackbough Swim: Thanks for sending that over! I've checked and the Lydia Classic Medium Bottoms also run true to size and almost have the same fit as Thea Asymmetrical Cheeky Bottoms so I'd recommend getting the Large size as well. Please let me know if there's anything else I can assist you with.
oooh love to hear that! that's actually perfect - thank you so much !! glad to have asked ◡̈

send_to_cs: Shipping inquiries on a current order
For product or general inquiries you can't find the answer to, use PGI shortcut
escalate: No escalations for this client, send everything to CS
tone: Don't use the word "unfortunately" to discuss our appointment structure
Don't use the word "best" to reference a product (e.g., best ring, etc.)
templates: If cx are looking for an engagement ring and don't provide specifics, use LER shortcut
dos_and_donts: If the customer responds incorrectly to the journey, don't send jny temp, recommend items based on their selection instead
Cx can create their own engagement ring on the webiste by choosing a setting, stone and size
Cx can customize rings by working with designers who will use their ideas, drawings, images to create the ring they want
Don't disclose the locations of their labs/production facilities
drive_to_purchase: Be MORE SALESY, if the customer is showing intent to purchase then:
➜ Ask open-ended general questions such as style & material
➜ After asking open-ended questions, transition to specific questions such as center stone, etc.
➜ If the customer liked our recommendation, push for the sale by asking about payment options or when they hope to have the item
promo_and_exclusions: Please see here for current offer details: https://www.brilliantearth.com/promo-codes-and-offers/

escalation_preferences: For questions regarding order, service, shipping questions on a current order, post-purchase will go to cssupport@brilliantearth.com
For product or general inquiry questions, please contact info@brilliantearth.com
